title: New Hampshire man arrested in 1993 homicide of Maine woman
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Daniel Jolly, 59, was arrested in Portsmouth, N.H. for the 1993 murder of Maxine Bitomski in Kittery, Maine.
The victim, Maxine Bitomski, was discovered dead in her home on Jan. 16, 1993, with the cause of death undisclosed.
Jolly, connected to Bitomski through his job at Medical Market, will be extradited to Maine for trial.

title: Red Sox demote Brayan Bello after another disastrous start
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox demoted pitcher Brayan Bello to Triple-A Worcester after another poor starting performance against the Orioles.
Bello's first-inning ERA reached 16.88 this season, contributing to an 8-2 loss to Baltimore.
Bello, despite a strong previous season, struggled as a starter in 2026 with a 9.68 ERA across seven starts.

title: Mass. police department decries dangerous behavior on e-bikes after 3 youth charged
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Attleboro police report 143 incidents of dangerous e-bike use by teens since November 2024.
A 16-year-old is charged with reckless e-bike operation and other offenses after a police encounter.
Two teens charged with attempted break-in to retrieve a confiscated e-bike from police storage.

title: Keep a look out for the Northern Lights over New England skies Thursday into Friday
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The aurora borealis may be visible over New England Thursday night into Friday morning, according to the NOAA.
Peak viewing hours for the northern lights in New England will be between 10 p.m. and 2 a.m. Thursday.
Coronal mass ejections from the sun are causing the geomagnetic storms that could make the aurora visible.

title: Am I required to take my new vehicle to the dealer for maintenance?
author: John Paul
bullets: Owners are not required to return to dealers for routine maintenance to maintain warranty coverage on new vehicles.
For maintenance, ensure correct oil specifications and quality filters are used; keep detailed records of maintenance.
Dealers offer benefits like vehicle familiarity and checking recalls, but trusted mechanics can perform maintenance if requirements are met.

title: Worcester man gets 4 to 7 years in prison for killing neighbor; family calls sentence a ‘slap in the face’
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Enrique Canales-Martinez sentenced to 4-7 years for manslaughter of neighbor Jason Mayhew in Worcester.
Dispute over loud music led to deadly shooting; Canales-Martinez pleaded guilty to avoid lengthy court process.
Mayhew's family criticized sentence as too lenient, expressing devastation over his loss in victim impact statements.

title: Holyoke church cancels service, temporarily closes after theft
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: St. Paul's Episcopal Church in Holyoke canceled services and closed temporarily after theft of fundraiser items.
The theft, occurring between May 24-27, included food and raffle prizes for a church fundraiser.
New security measures have been implemented, and authorities are investigating the break-in with church cooperation.

title: NFL insider: ‘Good chance’ Patriots will sign Christian Gonzalez to huge contract extension
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots are likely to extend Christian Gonzalez's contract this offseason, according to NFL insider Ian Rapoport.
Gonzalez's current contract pays $2.2 million this season, rising to $18.1 million by 2027 under the fifth-year option.
Coach Mike Vrabel expects Gonzalez, who missed voluntary OTAs, to return for the Patriots' mandatory minicamp next week.

title: Police investigating suspected arson at Boston’s Museum of African American History
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston Police are investigating a suspected arson at the Museum of African American History in Beacon Hill.
A burned package with Juneteenth-themed puzzles was found outside the museum, being probed as a suspected hate crime.
Boston police shared images of a suspect, described as a white male, to seek public assistance in identification.

title: Woman, 69, killed in Swampscott fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 69-year-old woman died after a fire in her Swampscott apartment, according to local authorities.
The fire occurred in a condominium complex along Loring Avenue, and the woman's identity is pending family notification.
The incident is under investigation by State Police, Swampscott Police and Fire Department, and the State Fire Marshal.
